issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle button does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close/toggle functions change the visual state but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users will not hear whether the menu is open or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open and close functions modify CSS classes but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive the expanded/collapsed state of dropdown panels, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1924: In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
        Line 1936: In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle switch (role="switch") changes visual state but does not update aria-pressed. Screen‑reader users cannot tell which state is active, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3070: In _syncToggleState, add: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, period === 'year');
        Line 3076: In init, set initial state: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, initPeriod === 'year');

issuesFound.title: product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the count of visible products changes dynamically, but no live‑region announcement is made. Screen‑reader users are not informed of the updated results, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2404: In applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: billing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/annual billing updates prices visually but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen‑reader users may not perceive the update, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3062: In setPeriod, after _updateSavingsMessage, add: liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: form validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator marks fields invalid with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen‑reader users are not informed of the error state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3844: In _renderResult, inside the else block (when result.valid is false), add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); and in the valid block add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load when the drawer is closed. Screen‑reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1856: In init, after _trigger is assigned, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen‑reader users are not informed that the panels are initially collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: In register, after setting aria-haspopup, add: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen-reader users will not be informed of the menu's state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: In open(), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 3015: In close(), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Line 3030: In init(), set initial state: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide dropdown panels without updating aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Screen-reader users cannot perceive the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdowns, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: In _openInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 3165: In _closeInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands an FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, while the close() function sets it to false. This inconsistent state management breaks screen-reader announcements of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: In open(), add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the product count badge but does not announce the new result count to screen readers. Users with visual impairments will not know when the filter has been applied and how many items are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In the callback after filterAnimation.run(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates prices and UI but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Monthly billing selected") to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology may miss the context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: In setPeriod(), after updating the UI, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3700: In the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Line 3715: In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set an initial aria-expanded state on the trigger button. The mobile menu is closed by default, so the trigger should have aria-expanded="false" from the start to provide correct state information to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3045: In init(), after setting up the trigger, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function registers dropdown triggers but does not set an initial aria-expanded="false" on them. Since dropdowns are closed by default, screen-reader users will not have accurate state information until after the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3175: In the register() function, after setting up the trigger, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on .hero‑ctas .btn‑primary:hover (translateY) missing reduced‑motion override

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on .hero‑image img (transform transition) missing reduced‑motion override

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button that opens and closes the mobile drawer never receives an `aria‑expanded` state. Assistive technologies cannot know whether the navigation is currently expanded, violating WCAG 2.1 (ARIA 1.1) requirements for toggles.

issuesFound.title: Search input does not reflect open/closed state with aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The combobox used for site search never updates `aria‑expanded` when the suggestion list is shown or hidden. Users of assistive technology cannot know whether results are available. This breaches the combobox pattern.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button lacks aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The element `[data-billing-toggle]` acts as a two‑state switch (monthly ↔ annual) but never receives an `aria‑pressed` value, so its state is invisible to screen readers. This violates the ARIA button pattern.

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons do not expose aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tabs are toggled with CSS classes only; they never receive `aria‑pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which filter is active. This violates the ARIA button/checkbox pattern for toggle groups.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle does not announce changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod method updates the price text, period label, and savings badge on all plan cards, but it never writes a message to a live‑region (role="status" or aria‑live) after the UI changes. Users of assistive technology are not informed when the billing period switches (e.g., from monthly to annual).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form binds inline validation to the email input via inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required','email']). The inlineValidator only adds error messages and CSS classes; it does not set aria‑invalid="true" on the input when validation fails, nor does it remove the attribute when the field becomes valid. This prevents screen readers from perceiving the error state.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” field validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form creates inputs for full name, email, subject, message, and consent checkbox, each bound with inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules). As with the newsletter form, inlineValidator does not set aria‑invalid on these inputs when they are invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Modify inlineValidator to set aria‑invalid="true" on the field when result.valid is false, and remove the attribute when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger (`.nav-toggle`) opens and closes the navigation drawer, but the code never sets or updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the button. Assistive technologies therefore cannot know whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.open()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. If the button is a `<button>` the attribute can be set directly; otherwise use `setAttribute`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger elements lack aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: In the dropdown‑menu component each trigger button receives `aria-haspopup="true"` but the script never toggles `aria-expanded` when the panel is opened or closed. Screen readers therefore cannot announce the open/closed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Ensure the attribute is removed or set to `"false"` on initial load.  

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period UI (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is treated as a switch (role = "switch") but the code never updates the `aria‑pressed` state when the user toggles between monthly and annual billing. This leaves the switch’s state invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the period in `setPeriod()` (or in `_onToggleClick()`), call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`. Also initialise the attribute on page load to match the default period.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult()` only adds CSS classes (`field‑invalid` / `field‑valid`) and updates error‑message elements, but it never sets the `aria‑invalid` attribute on the input when validation fails. Consequently, screen‑reader users receive no programmatic indication that a field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown triggers receive `aria-haspopup="true"` but are never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so screen readers may assume the menu is expanded before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix: In `dropdownMenu.register(trigger, panel)` after setting `aria-haspopup`, also set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. This attribute should be toggled to `"true"` when the panel opens (see Issue 2).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global reset
issuesFound.explanation: The universal selector rule removes the default focus outline from all interactive elements without providing a visible replacement. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (AA) and 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA 2.2), preventing keyboard users from tracking focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 102: *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
    Line 103: *:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

Line 300: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onPointerDown` (lines 10213–10219) and `_onPointerUp` (lines 10221–10237) are attached to a non-native interactive element (the carousel root) via `addEventListener('pointerdown', ...)` and `addEventListener('pointerup', ...)`. These handlers implement swipe detection but do not provide matching keyboard handlers for Enter and Space, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard). Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe actions.

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the `is-open` class and update the DOM but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change of the mobile navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10057: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10064: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the mobile navigation is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as the initial state is not programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10085: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` change the navigation menu's visibility but do not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers will not know if the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10057: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    Line 10064: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` adds error classes and messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. This violates WCAG 3.3.1, as screen readers cannot announce the field's invalid state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` does not clear `aria-invalid="false"` when a field becomes valid. This violates WCAG 3.3.1, as the invalid state may persist incorrectly.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in `pricing` toggles the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen readers cannot announce the toggle state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add the following after line 10520:
```
setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(next === 'year'));
```

fpTitles: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module toggles the CSS class `is-open` on the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu has opened or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4840: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 4841: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the `close` function

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and scroll locking but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the navigation trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialisation does not set aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function discovers the trigger element but does not initialise its aria-expanded attribute to "false". The initial state is ambiguous to assistive technologies until the first interaction.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function fails to activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable but never initialises createFocusTrap or calls activate() when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not contained within the drawer, allowing users to tab into obscured page content.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide the panel but do not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users are not notified of the dropdown state change.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu registration does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register() function sets aria-haspopup but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial closed state is not programmatically defined.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function sets the item state to open and animates the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close() function correctly sets "false", creating an inconsistent state management.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle sync does not update aria-pressed or aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing _syncToggleState() function updates visual classes for billing period buttons but does not update aria-pressed (for buttons) or aria-checked (for switches). The selected state is not accessible.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _updateCountBadge() function updates the text content and aria-label of the count badge but does not announce the change via a live region. Users are not informed when the product count changes after filtering.

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message update not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid

issuesFound.fix: Line 549: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) and transition effects without a reduced-motion override, which can trigger vestibular issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 566: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on product cards
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use transform: translateY(-3px) on hover without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which can cause discomfort for users with motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 721: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Global link underline removal without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The universal a selector removes text underlines without providing an alternative visual distinction (such as border-bottom or background), making it difficult for users to identify links, especially those with color vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border-color. This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements relying on the default browser focus ring, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Search button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets outline: none on focus-visible but only provides outline-offset without a visible outline style, color, or alternative box-shadow. This leaves keyboard users without a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on focus-visible and only sets outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu links focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links remove the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset defined, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible and only set outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Feature card links focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to select the checkbox.

issuesFound.title: Screen reader utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible to sighted users and removes it from the accessibility tree in some contexts. It should use position: absolute with 1px dimensions to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 147:
  word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, even though the menu starts in a closed state. This violates the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1600: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;` and the event listener attachments.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but does not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. The setExpanded helper function exists in the codebase but is not used in these toggle functions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1668: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 1683: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but fails to initialise aria-expanded="false" for the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item dynamically, the function adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an asymmetry where the state is only announced when closing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2648: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as a toggle group where one button is always pressed (active category), but the code only toggles CSS classes and does not update the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the pressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2290: Replace the existing forEach block with:
  `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); });`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count changes and cards animate in/out, but there is no announcement via an ARIA live region to inform screen reader users how many results are now showing or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2169: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);` after the emit call inside the filterAnimation.run callback.

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually with animation, but the change in billing period and prices is not announced to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after the requestAnimationFrame that resets the animating flag.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements for expand/collapse
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen reader users. While the aria-expanded attribute is updated (or should be), the context of which item was opened and its title is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2677: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));` before the emit call in the open function.
  Line 2703: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));` before the emit call in the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, there is no announcement to screen reader users that the menu state has changed, which can be disorienting when navigating via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1674: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` inside _openInstance after the emit call.
  Line 1690: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` inside _closeInstance after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to communicate the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1017: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid cleanup
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as valid, the function updates aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1028: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the start of the function before the if block.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded updates on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input implements the combobox pattern but does not update aria-expanded when the suggestion listbox opens or closes. Additionally, the initial state is not set.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2048: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _open after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 2059: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _close after `_searchState.open = false;`
  Line 1978: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside the init function after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers lack Enter/Space keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: The keyboard handler for FAQ items handles arrow key navigation but does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate (toggle) the FAQ item when the trigger is not a native button element (e.g., a div with role="button"). This prevents keyboard users from activating items.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2728: Add cases for Enter and Space before the default case:
  `case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: { e.preventDefault(); const item = faqRegistry.getAll().find(i => i.trigger === trigger); if (item) toggle(item); break; }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded update in close function
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3154: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `_open = false;` within the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded update in _openInstance
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element (inst.trigger), violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3280: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the _openInstance function, before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded update in _closeInstance
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3295: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the beginning of the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-controls and aria-haspopup but omits the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger button, failing to communicate the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3320: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the register function, after the line setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded update in _open
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open function displays the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the search input (_inputEl), which has role="combobox". Screen readers cannot detect that the popup is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3650: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of the _open function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded update in _close
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._close function hides the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the popup is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3660: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the _close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function configures the input as a combobox but never sets the initial aria-expanded="false" state, violating the ARIA combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3710: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the init function, after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded update in open function
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel and sets item.open = true, but fails to update the trigger's aria-expanded attribute to "true", breaking the accordion accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4120: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` within the open function.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates the visual active state of billing period buttons using CSS classes but does not set aria-pressed to reflect the pressed state for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4350: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the _periodBtns.forEach loop in the setPeriod function, after the toggleClass call.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.init function discovers period buttons and syncs the toggle state, but does not initialise aria-pressed attributes on the buttons when the module initialises.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4420: Add `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-period') === initPeriod)); });` after the initPeriod is determined in the init function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function toggles the active CSS class on filter tab buttons but does not update aria-pressed to indicate which tab is currently active/pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4550: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop in _syncTabAriaStates, after the toggleClass call.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.init function calls _syncTabAriaStates but does not ensure aria-pressed is set on the initial active tab button, leaving the initial pressed state unknown to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4620: Add `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory)); });` after _syncTabAriaStates() is called in init.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field, preventing screen readers from identifying the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` at the end of the markInvalid function, after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2760: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and count badge but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 12 products") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4500: Add `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === productCardRegistry.count() ? STRINGS.filterAll : STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));` after the emit call in the filterAnimation.run callback.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates prices and billing labels dynamically but does not announce the period change (e.g., "Switched to annual billing") to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4370: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after the emit call in the setPeriod function.

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismiss lacks pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager.show function creates auto-dismissing toasts using setTimeout but provides no mechanism to pause, stop, or hide the timer on mouse hover or focus, violating WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2890: Add mouseenter and focusin listeners to pause the timer, and mouseleave/focusout to resume: `toast.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => clearTimeout(timerId)); toast.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { if (dur > 0) timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); });` (and similar for focusin/focusout).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function makes the drawer visible but does not activate a focus trap to keep keyboard navigation within the drawer, allowing focus to escape to the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3130: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` after _lockScroll() in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module displays the navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the state change to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1129: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves assistive technologies with stale state information, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `_open = false;` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The init() function does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the page loads. Since the menu is closed by default, the attribute must be present to indicate the initial state, per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1164: Insert `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `_initialised = true;` inside the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel but does not update aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1236: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` inside the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides the dropdown panel but does not update aria-expanded to "false" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies will continue to report the menu as expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `inst.open = false;` inside the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function sets up dropdown triggers but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the panel is hidden on page load. The attribute must reflect the initial closed state, per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1264: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` inside the register function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() function displays the search suggestions panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element. Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1354: Insert `if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the end of the _open function (before the closing brace).

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _close() function hides the search suggestions panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input element, leaving assistive technologies with stale state information, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1361: Insert `if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the end of the _close function (before the closing brace).

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mount() function in the suggestionListRenderer module (called during siteSearch init) sets up the combobox input but does not initialise aria-expanded="false". Since the suggestion list is hidden by default, the attribute must be present initially, per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1276: Insert `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` inside the mount function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands the FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close() function correctly sets it to "false", the asymmetry means the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1552: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function applies visual validation styles and manages aria-describedby, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear aria-invalid on valid fields. Assistive technologies cannot detect validation errors without this attribute, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1634: Inside the `if (result.valid)` block, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` (or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`). Inside the `else` block, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for filter results
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the product grid and emits a custom event, but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 12 products") to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the content has changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: Insert `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);` immediately after the line `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside the applyFilters function.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates pricing display and emits a custom event when the billing period changes, but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers. Users receive no confirmation of the state change, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1484: Insert `liveRegion.announce(\`Billing period changed to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}\`);` immediately before the `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange'...)` line inside the setPeriod function.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function updates the visual active state of billing period buttons but does not update aria-pressed to reflect which button is selected. Since these buttons act as a toggle group, the pressed state must be conveyed to assistive technologies, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function updates the visual active class on filter tab buttons but does not update aria-pressed. These buttons behave as toggle buttons indicating the active filter, so the pressed state must be communicated to screen readers, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1417: Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is broken — content not hidden from sight
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — the exact opposite of what a screen-reader-only utility should do. This makes content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technology. Screen reader users may miss important content if developers rely on this class expecting it to work correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 273-282: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text harder to read, particularly for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision, as it reduces the visual separation between words.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition with perspective/rotateY/rotateX effects, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable or reduce this motion. Users who have set their system to prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D rotation animation, which can cause vestibular discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 603-606: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Button active transform missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active rule applies transform: translateY(1px) with a transition on transform, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the button press animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 329-330: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without sufficient visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links globally. For body text links outside of navigation and button contexts, there is no alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or background highlight on hover) to distinguish links from surrounding text. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as color alone is used to convey that text is a link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 147: a { text-decoration: none; } .prose a { text-decoration: underline; } .prose a:hover { text-decoration-thickness: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Base font size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html rule sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) base size for body text. Small font sizes reduce readability for users with low vision, cognitive disabilities, or reading difficulties. All rem-based sizes throughout the stylesheet will be calculated from this 12px base, making text smaller than intended.

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below recommended minimum

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is completely broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — the exact opposite of what a screen-reader-only class should do. Content meant to be hidden from sighted users but announced to screen readers will be fully visible, breaking the intended accessibility pattern. Skip links, form labels, and other sr-only content will not be hidden.

issuesFound.title: Hero image 3D transform missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a 3D transform (perspective, rotateY, rotateX) and transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at lines 605-610 is empty and does not disable the transform. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D rotation effect, which can cause vestibular discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 605-610: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Skip link transform animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with a transition to animate into view on focus. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion) override to disable this animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the slide-in animation.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 358: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: translateY(-200%); transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card hover transform missing prefers-reduced-motion override

Due to CSS specificity, the featured card transform will still apply even when reduced motion is preferred.
issuesFound.fix: Line 818-820: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not move focus into drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not programmatically moved into the drawer. Keyboard and screen reader users may remain focused on the trigger button without awareness that new content has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2634: add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the navigation order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2658: add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2117: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but never clears aria-invalid from the field. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2138: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the aria-describedby cleanup block.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() function updates the visible product count but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users of assistive technology are not informed when filtering changes the number of visible products.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3479: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count) || \`Showing \${count} products\`);` after the aria-label update.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render does not announce changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The render() function adds and removes filter chips from the DOM but does not announce these changes to screen readers. Users of assistive technology are not informed when filters are added or removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3742: add `liveRegion.announce(\`Filter \${c.label} \${chips.length > 0 ? 'applied' : 'removed'}\`);` after the chips.forEach loop.

fpTitles: activeFilterChips render does not announce changes to screen readers

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen reader users are not notified that the field contains invalid input.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description reference but never clears aria-invalid from the field. Screen reader users may continue to hear that the field is invalid even after correction.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up the mobile navigation but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The initial collapsed state is not communicated to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger but never initializes aria-expanded="false". The initial collapsed state is not communicated to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in mobileNav adds CSS classes to show the drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the navigation menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function in mobileNav removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state of the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not activate focus trap when drawer opens
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module creates a focus trap via createFocusTrap() in init() but never activates it in the open() function. Keyboard users can Tab outside the drawer when it is open, violating WCAG 2.4.3.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function shows the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the dropdown is expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the dropdown is collapsed.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce that the field has a validation error, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description reference but never clears aria-invalid from the field. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid even after the error is resolved.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function sets up event listeners but never sets the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button. Screen readers have no initial state information for the navigation toggle.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function sets aria-haspopup on the trigger but never sets the initial aria-expanded="false" state. Screen readers have no initial state information for dropdown triggers.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The label element in FormGroup does not have an htmlFor attribute to programmatically associate it with its corresponding input. Screen reader users cannot click the label to focus the input, and the association is not conveyed programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 649: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an invalid prop but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen reader users are not notified when the field has a validation error.

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component receives an invalid prop but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the textarea element. Screen reader users are not notified when the field has a validation error.

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component receives an invalid prop but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the select element. Screen reader users are not notified when the field has a validation error.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
